title: dactylorhiza fuchsii var. cornubiensis
intro: Dactylorhiza fuchsii var. cornubiensis, also known as the Cornish marsh orchid, is a rare and endemic variety of the autumn lady's tresses, a type of orchid native to the UK. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza fuchsii var. cornubiensis are still being researched, but it is believed to have antioxidant and cardiovascular-protective properties. In herbal preparations, the plant can be dried and used as a powder or made into capsules or teas, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential side effects.
